learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements
outcomes: Understand the need for measurement and systems of units including fundamental and derived units.
Apply international standards for SI units, symbols, and nomenclature of physical quantities.
Determine significant figures and calculate uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis and apply it to solve problems.
Define frame of reference and describe motion in a straight line.
Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe motion.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity.
Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods (v-t, x-t) and calculus-based relations.
Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform vector addition, subtraction, and resolution.
Calculate scalar and vector products of vectors.
Analyze cases of uniform velocity and acceleration in a plane including projectile motion.
Explain intuitive concepts of force, inertia, Newton's laws (1st, 2nd, 3rd), impulse, and linear momentum conservation.
Analyze equilibrium of concurrent forces including static, kinetic, rolling friction, and lubrication.
Apply dynamics principles to uniform circular motion, centripetal force, banked roads, and level curves.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power
outcomes: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces.
Apply the work-energy theorem involving kinetic energy and power.
Analyze conservative vs. non-conservative forces, potential energy (including springs), and motion in vertical circles.
Evaluate elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions.
